title: Apply Deny-by-Exception Policy to Prevent Use of Unauthorized Software
description: Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) policy to prevent use of unauthorized software or deny-all, permit-by-exception (whitelisting) policy to allow execution of authorized software.

checks.name: AppLocker or WDAC Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC) is configured and enforcing an allow or deny policy for executable files

checks.name: AppLocker or WDAC Policy is in Enforce Mode
checks.purpose: Confirm the application control policy is in Enforce mode and not Audit-only mode

checks.name: Application Control Rules Cover Executables, Scripts, and Installers
checks.purpose: Verify application control rules cover EXE, DLL, Script, and MSI rule collections to prevent bypass

checks.name: AppLocker or WDAC Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify AppLocker or WDAC is configured and enforcing an allow or deny policy on this server

checks.name: AppLocker or WDAC Policy is in Enforce Mode
checks.purpose: Confirm the application control policy is in Enforce mode and not Audit-only mode

checks.name: Application Control Rules Cover Executables, Scripts, and Installers
checks.purpose: Verify application control rules cover EXE, DLL, Script, and MSI rule collections

checks.name: Application Whitelisting or Execution Control is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify SELinux, AppArmor, or an equivalent MAC framework is enforcing execution control policies

checks.name: SELinux or AppArmor is in Enforcing Mode
checks.purpose: Confirm SELinux is set to Enforcing or AppArmor profiles are in enforce mode and not disabled or permissive

checks.name: No Unauthorized SUID or SGID Binaries Exist
checks.purpose: Scan the filesystem for SUID/SGID binaries and verify all found binaries are in the approved list

checks.name: Application Whitelisting or Execution Control is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify AppArmor is installed and enforcing execution control profiles on this Debian system

checks.name: AppArmor is in Enforcing Mode
checks.purpose: Confirm AppArmor profiles are loaded and in enforce mode and not in complain or disabled state

checks.name: No Unauthorized SUID or SGID Binaries Exist
checks.purpose: Scan the filesystem for SUID/SGID binaries and verify all found binaries are in the approved list

remediation: Deploy and enforce AppLocker or WDAC policies on Windows covering EXE, DLL, Script, and MSI rule collections. Set policies to Enforce mode. On Linux, enable and enforce SELinux or AppArmor. Audit and remediate unauthorized SUID/SGID binaries. Maintain an approved application and binary list.
